diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..002fc6b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,160 @@
+# staging-infra
+
+Shared staging infrastructure for Gaia services.
+
+This repository owns the common platform layer:
+- Traefik ingress (host-based routing)
+- Cloudflare Tunnel exposure (no inbound router ports)
+- Shared PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, RabbitMQ, and ChromaDB
+- Backup and restore scripts
+- Self-hosted GitHub Actions deploy workflow
+
+## 1) Prerequisites on server
+
+- Docker Engine + Compose plugin installed and running
+- GitHub Actions self-hosted runner configured on this machine with label `staging`
+- Cloudflare Tunnel credentials file available at `cloudflared/credentials.json`
+
+## 2) Bootstrap
+
+```bash
+./scripts/bootstrap.sh
+```
+
+Edit `env/core.env` and `cloudflared/config.yml` with real values.
+
+## 3) Deploy core stack
+
+```bash
+./scripts/preflight.sh
+./scripts/deploy.sh
+```
+
+Note: this deploys internal core services. To include Cloudflare Tunnel exposure, run:
+
+```bash
+ENABLE_PUBLIC=1 ./scripts/deploy.sh
+```
+
+Optional (with observability):
+
+```bash
+ENABLE_OBSERVABILITY=1 ./scripts/deploy.sh
+```
+
+## 4) Onboard each app (DB + Redis ACL)
+
+```bash
+cp env/postgres/_template.env.example env/postgres/<app-name>.env
+# Fill values in env/postgres/<app-name>.env
+./scripts/provision-app.sh <app-name>
+```
+
+`provision-app.sh` creates:
+- PostgreSQL role + database for that app
+- MongoDB user in shared `GAIA` database (app-scoped user)
+- Redis ACL user scoped to app key prefix (includes `INFO` for ARQ startup checks and `KEYS` for cache pattern invalidation)
+- RabbitMQ user + vhost permissions for that app
+- Chroma collection prefix contract for that app
+
+`onboard-app.sh` remains as a compatibility alias to `provision-app.sh`.
+
+To remove app-scoped resources:
+
+```bash
+./scripts/deprovision-app.sh <app-name>
+```
+
+## 5) Connect app repositories

# Per-PR preview stack template.
# Rendered by scripts/render-preview.sh with env substitution.
# Requires env vars:
#   PR_NUMBER          — numeric PR number (used in project + container names)
#   API_IMAGE          — ghcr.io/.../gaia-api:pr-{N}-{sha}
#   WEB_IMAGE          — ghcr.io/.../gaia-web:pr-{N}-{sha}
#   PUBLIC_HOST        — your-host.ts.net
#   PUBLIC_ORIGIN      — https://{PUBLIC_HOST}
#   APP_ENV_FILE       — absolute path to rendered per-PR api env
#   WEB_ENV_FILE       — absolute path to rendered per-PR web env
#   CORE_NETWORK       — shared docker network (default: gaia_core_net)

name: gaia-pr-${PR_NUMBER}

services:
  api:
    image: ${API_IMAGE}
    container_name: gaia-pr-${PR_NUMBER}-api
    restart: unless-stopped
    env_file:
      - ${APP_ENV_FILE}
    environment:
      ENV: development
      INFISICAL_ENV: staging
      PREVIEW_APP_NAME: pr-${PR_NUMBER}
      UV_NO_SYNC: "1"
    command:
      - python
      - -m
      - uvicorn
      - app.main:app
      - --host
      - 0.0.0.0
      - --port
      - "8000"
      - --no-access-log
    networks:
      - core
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "python", "-c", "import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://127.0.0.1:8000/health', timeout=5)"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 60s

  worker:
    image: ${API_IMAGE}
    container_name: gaia-pr-${PR_NUMBER}-worker
    restart: unless-stopped
    env_file:

#   PR_NUMBER          — numeric PR number (used in project + container names)
#   API_IMAGE          — ghcr.io/.../gaia-api:pr-{N}-{sha}
#   WEB_IMAGE          — ghcr.io/.../gaia-web:pr-{N}-{sha}
#   PUBLIC_HOST        — your-host.ts.net
#   PUBLIC_ORIGIN      — https://{PUBLIC_HOST}
#   APP_ENV_FILE       — absolute path to rendered per-PR api env
#   WEB_ENV_FILE       — absolute path to rendered per-PR web env
#   CORE_NETWORK       — shared docker network (default: gaia_core_net)

name: gaia-pr-${PR_NUMBER}

services:
  api:
    image: ${API_IMAGE}
    container_name: gaia-pr-${PR_NUMBER}-api
    restart: unless-stopped
    env_file:
      - ${APP_ENV_FILE}
    environment:
      ENV: development
      INFISICAL_ENV: staging
      PREVIEW_APP_NAME: pr-${PR_NUMBER}
      UV_NO_SYNC: "1"
    command:
      - python
      - -m
      - uvicorn
      - app.main:app
      - --host
      - 0.0.0.0
      - --port
      - "8000"
      - --no-access-log
    networks:
      - core
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "python", "-c", "import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://127.0.0.1:8000/health', timeout=5)"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 60s

  worker:
    image: ${API_IMAGE}
    container_name: gaia-pr-${PR_NUMBER}-worker
    restart: unless-stopped
    env_file:

content.content:
"""Write staging-infra/previews/index.html listing all active PR previews."""
    from datetime import datetime

    from previewctl.docker_ops import DockerPreviewSpec, docker_stack_status
    from previewctl.port_pool import PortPool

    pool = PortPool(cfg.runtime_dir / "docker-port-pool.json")
    allocations = pool.list_allocations()

    entries: list[dict] = []
    for pr_num, port in sorted(allocations.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]):
        is_develop = pr_num == project.develop_pr_number
        slug = "develop" if is_develop else f"pr-{pr_num}"
        url = f"{project.public_scheme}://{public_host}:{port}"
        sha = (deployed.get(pr_num) or "")[:7] or "?"

        spec = DockerPreviewSpec(
            pr_number=pr_num,
            api_image="",
            web_image="",
            staging_infra_dir=cfg.staging_infra_dir,
            runtime_dir=cfg.runtime_dir,
            public_base_domain="",
            public_scheme=project.public_scheme,
            core_network=cfg.compose_network,
        )
        raw = docker_stack_status(spec)
        health: dict[str, str] = {}
        for name, status in raw.items():
            svc = name.rsplit("-", 1)[-1]
            sl = status.lower()
            if "unhealthy" in sl or "exited" in sl:
                health[svc] = "unhealthy"
            elif "healthy" in sl:
                health[svc] = "healthy"
            elif sl.startswith("up"):
                health[svc] = "running"
            else:
                health[svc] = "stopped"

        meta = (pr_meta or {}).get(pr_num, {})
        entries.append(
            {
                "pr_number": pr_num,
                "slug": slug,
                "url": url,
                "port": port,
                "sha": sha,
                "is_develop": is_develop,
                "health": health,
                "title": meta.get("title", ""),
                "head_ref": meta.get("head_ref", ""),
                "user_login": meta.get("user_login", ""),
                "user_avatar": meta.get("user_avatar", ""),
            }
        )

    ts = datetime.now(UTC).strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
    repo_slug = getattr(project, "repo_slug", "") or "theexperiencecompany/gaia"
    html = _render_previews_html(entries, ts, repo_slug=repo_slug)
    out_dir = cfg.staging_infra_dir / "previews"
    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

aiTitle: Debug port 8102 access after Cloudflare migration

content.text:
Now I can answer honestly. Diagnosis was right; let me reframe it accurately for you.

## "It was working before" — really?

I went deep on the git history of both `gaia-previewctl` and `staging-infra`. Tell me which of these you remember:

- **Did you ever click an "Open" button on `/previews` and see a real PR app load?** Or did you just see green dots and assume?
- **Were you running previews locally via `previewctl` with SSH port-forwards to `127.0.0.1:300X`?** That code path exists (`_preview_url_for_slot` → `http://127.0.0.1:{api_port}`) and would have actually worked.
- **Was there an older setup before April 26?** That's when `staging-infra` was first committed.

Because here's what the history actually says — **the dashboard URLs (`https://…:810N`) have never resolved**, ever:

| Date | Commit | What it did | What it *didn't* do |
|---|---|---|---|
| Apr 26 | `1a3ba7e` | Added Traefik entryPoints `pr8101`–`pr8120`. Commit message says "with TLS." | Never added Traefik labels to `preview-template.yml`. Never enabled TLS on those entryPoints. |
| Apr 26 | `2a04e39` | Published ports 8101–8120, mounted `traefik/certs:ro`. Comment: *"so Tailscale clients can reach https://hostname:810x directly with TLS."* | Never populated `certs/`. Never added `tls.yml` dynamic config. |
| ~May 1 | `a3a3f3e` | Generated `/previews` index linking to `https://host:810N`. | Those links pointed to URLs that had no router, no TLS, nothing. |

I grepped the entire history of both repos for `traefik.http.routers` in any compose label — **zero matches, ever**. The certs directory has been empty since it was mounted. So when you hit `:8102`:
- TLS: no cert, no entrypoint TLS → handshake fails (your "https doesn't work")
- HTTP: Traefik listens but has no routers → Go's `http.NotFound` (your `http://` 404)

This is a half-shipped feature, not a regression. **Cloudflare didn't break it. You didn't break it. It was launched broken** — probably because nobody clicked "Open" until now.

## How to prevent it next time

Three things, in order of impact:

1. **Smoke test in `docker-up.sh`.** After `docker compose up -d` and the API healthcheck, curl `https://host:${PR_PORT}/health` and fail loudly on anything that isn't 200. The current script only checks the *container* is healthy — not that Traefik can route to it. That gap is exactly how this shipped.
2. **CI on `staging-infra`.** Render a sample `preview-template.yml` and `traefik validate` (or `traefik --configFile=... --dry-run`) — Traefik will warn about routers referring to non-existent entryPoints, missing TLS stores, etc.
3. **Be skeptical of commit messages that claim "with TLS."** The `2a04e39` and `1a3ba7e` messages described a finished system; the diffs delivered a skeleton. A reviewer testing the actual URL would have caught it.

## So — do I keep going with the patch?

diff --git a/src/previewctl/cli.py b/src/previewctl/cli.py
index 87541dd..83750da 100644
--- a/src/previewctl/cli.py
+++ b/src/previewctl/cli.py
@@ -3164,6 +3164,38 @@ def docker_poll(
         short = sha[:7]
         return f"gaia-staging/{service}:{slug}-{short}"
 
+    def _logs_url(pr_number: int) -> str | None:
+        # Dozzle filter URL — points reviewers at the per-PR containers.
+        origin = (project.public_origin or "").rstrip("/")
+        if not origin:
+            return None
+        return f"{origin}/logs?filter=name%3Dgaia-pr-{pr_number}-"
+
+    def _report(
+        pr_number: int,
+        sha: str,
+        phase: str,
+        repo_slug: str,
+        url: str | None = None,
+        error: str | None = None,
+        started_at: float | None = None,
+    ) -> None:
+        # Wrapper so the entire status-reporting path is fail-silent —
+        # we never want a GitHub API hiccup to break a deploy loop.
+        try:
+            github_status.report(
+                repo_slug=repo_slug,
+                pr_number=pr_number,
+                sha=sha,
+                phase=phase,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
+                url=url,
+                logs_url=_logs_url(pr_number),
+                error=error,
+                started_at=started_at,
+            )
+        except Exception as exc:
+            typer.echo(f"[{datetime.now().isoformat()}] pr={pr_number} status report failed: {exc}")
+
     def _run_once() -> None:
         with _project_env(project):
             cfg = load_config()
@@ -3216,16 +3248,33 @@ def docker_poll(
                     / "worktrees"
                     / ("develop" if is_develop else f"pr-{pr_num}")
                 )
+                phase_started_at = time.time()
+                actual_sha = sha  # provisional until rev-parse confirms
                 try:
                     old_sha = deployed.get(pr_num)
+                    _report(pr_num, sha, "queued", repo_slug, started_at=phase_started_at)
                     _ensure_gaia_worktree(project.app_repo, worktree, branch_ref)
                     actual_sha = _run_cmd(["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"], worktree)
                     api_tag = _image_tag(pr_num, "api", actual_sha)
                     web_tag = _image_tag(pr_num, "web", actual_sha)
 
                     if build:
+                        _report(
+                            pr_num,
+                            actual_sha,
+                            "building",
+                            repo_slug,
+                            started_at=phase_started_at,
+                        )
                         _docker_build_images(worktree, pr_num, branch_ref, api_tag, web_tag)
 
+                    _report(
+                        pr_num,
+                        actual_sha,
+                        "deploying",
+                        repo_slug,
+                        started_at=phase_started_at,
+                    )
                     url = _docker_deploy_pr(
                         cfg, pr_num, actual_sha, api_tag, web_tag, public_host_resolved
                     )
@@ -3233,6 +3282,14 @@ def docker_poll(
                     typer.echo(
                         f"[{datetime.now().isoformat()}] pr={pr_num} sha={actual_sha[:7]} url={url}"
                     )
+                    _report(
+                        pr_num,
+                        actual_sha,
+                        "success",
+                        repo_slug,
+                        url=url,
+                        started_at=phase_started_at,
+                    )
                     # Remove previous images now that the new stack is up
                     if old_sha and old_sha != actual_sha:
                         _docker_remove_images(
@@ -3243,6 +3300,14 @@ def docker_poll(
                         )
                 except Exception as exc:
                     typer.echo(f"[{datetime.now().isoformat()}] pr={pr_num} deploy failed: {exc}")
+                    _report(
+                        pr_num,
+                        actual_sha,
+                        "failed",
+                        repo_slug,
+                        error=str(exc),
+                        started_at=phase_started_at,
+                    )
 
             # --- Tear down stale previews ---
             from previewctl.port_pool import PortPool
@@ -3265,10 +3330,18 @@ def docker_poll(
                                     _image_tag(pr_num, "web", old_sha),
                                 ]
                             )
+                        _report(pr_num, old_sha or "", "torndown", repo_slug)
                     except Exception as exc:
                         typer.echo(
                             f"[{datetime.now().isoformat()}] pr={pr_num} teardown failed: {exc}"
                         )
+                        _report(

+                            "failed",

===origin-readme===
# staging-infra

Shared staging infrastructure for Gaia services.

This repository owns the common platform layer:
- Traefik ingress (host-based routing)
- Cloudflare Tunnel exposure (no inbound router ports)
- Shared PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, RabbitMQ, and ChromaDB
- Backup and restore scripts
- Self-hosted GitHub Actions deploy workflow

## 1) Prerequisites on server

- Docker Engine + Compose plugin installed and running
- GitHub Actions self-hosted runner configured on this machine with label `staging`
- Cloudflare Tunnel credentials file available at `cloudflared/credentials.json`

## 2) Bootstrap

```bash
./scripts/bootstrap.sh
```

Edit `env/core.env` and `cloudflared/config.yml` with real values.

## 3) Deploy core stack

```bash
./scripts/preflight.sh
./scripts/deploy.sh
```

Note: this deploys internal core services. To include Cloudflare Tunnel exposure, run:

```bash
ENABLE_PUBLIC=1 ./scripts/deploy.sh
```

Optional (with observability):

```bash
ENABLE_OBSERVABILITY=1 ./scripts/deploy.sh
```

## 4) Onboard each app (DB + Redis ACL)

```bash
cp env/postgres/_template.env.example env/postgres/<app-name>.env
# Fill values in env/postgres/<app-name>.env
./scripts/provision-app.sh <app-name>
```

`provision-app.sh` creates:
- PostgreSQL role + database for that app
- MongoDB user in shared `GAIA` database (app-scoped user)
- Redis ACL user scoped to app key prefix (includes `INFO` for ARQ startup checks and `KEYS` for cache pattern invalidation)
- RabbitMQ user + vhost permissions for that app
- Chroma collection prefix contract for that app

`onboard-app.sh` remains as a compatibility alias to `provision-app.sh`.

aiTitle: Debug port 8102 access after Cloudflare migration

content.text:
OK I have enough to give you the straight answer. Stopping all patching. Here's what actually happened.

## Repo defaults vs what's on your server

The repos at `theexperiencecompany/staging-infra` + `gaia-previewctl` are NOT a "just clone and it works" thing — `cloudflared/config.yml`, `cloudflared/credentials.json`, `env/core.env`, `traefik/certs/*`, and `traefik/dynamic/tls.yml` are all **gitignored or excluded by design**. The README literally says *"Edit env/core.env and cloudflared/config.yml with real values."* So whatever you remember as "working great" required some local setup — it was never pure repo default.

## What's locally drifted from origin/main

**`staging-infra`** (branch up to date with `origin/main`, but with uncommitted changes):

| Change | What it is | Should it exist? |
|---|---|---|
| `compose/core.yml` modified: `/var/run/docker.sock` → `/run/user/1000/docker.sock` | Adapts the docker mount for rootless docker | **Required** — without it Traefik can't talk to Docker. But it's a local hack. |
| `traefik/dynamic/tls.yml` (untracked) | Tells Traefik to use `cert.pem`+`key.pem` as default cert for TLS routers | **Required** for `:8102` etc — but never committed |
| `traefik/certs/cert.pem` + `key.pem` (untracked) | Real Let's Encrypt cert for `gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net`, issued Apr 30, expires Jul 29 | **Required** — but never committed (correctly — secrets shouldn't be) |
| `traefik/dynamic/pr-*.yml` (untracked) | **Auto-generated by previewctl** every poll | Should be gitignored, never committed |
| `previews/index.html` (untracked) | Auto-generated dashboard | Should be gitignored, never committed |

**`gaia-previewctl`** is on local branch `feat/previews-table-layout` which is **7 commits behind origin/main**. Main has the `github-status` Deployment widget feature already merged (`60687d0`, `8bee930`, `ca476a9`). You're sitting on the un-rebased pre-merge branch with a half-applied copy of that same feature staged as uncommitted edits in `src/previewctl/cli.py`. Classic "I started this work, didn't finish, never pulled latest" state.

## Why `:8102` is actually broken right now

I verified each layer:

1. ✅ **previewctl writes correct Traefik routes.** `pr-706.yml` declares `entryPoints: [pr8102]`, both `/api` and `/` routers, `tls: {}`, pointing at the right container DNS names. Same for 8103/8104.
2. ✅ **The cert is valid.** Issued for `gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net`, not expired.
3. ✅ **`tls.yml` references the right files.** Cert + key paths are correct.
4. ❌ **Traefik never loaded `tls.yml` at runtime.** Direct probe with proper SNI returns `TLS alert: unrecognized name` — meaning Traefik does the TLS handshake but has no certificate matching the SNI in its TLS store.

The Traefik process has been running continuously since **May 26**. `tls.yml` was created **May 27 19:13** — *after* Traefik started. Even though the file provider has `watch: true`, in rootless docker with bind mounts, inotify on new files is unreliable. **Traefik has been running this whole time without ever loading the TLS config.**

That's it. That's the whole bug. **One `docker compose restart traefik` would fix all `:810N` URLs right now.**

The Porkbun→Cloudflare migration is unrelated. `.ts.net` doesn't touch Cloudflare at all. The host-based path (`https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net/previews`) keeps working because Tailscale Serve terminates TLS for that one, not Traefik.

## "Repo as source of truth" — what to actually do

You asked for two things: (1) revert local bullshit, (2) no manual Traefik configs. Those conflict — the per-PR port URLs require `tls.yml` + `certs/` *somewhere*. So you have to pick:

questions.question: Which direction matches what you actually want?

options.label: Just restart Traefik now, fix repo drift later (Recommended)
options.description: Step 1 (60s): you run `docker compose -f compose/core.yml restart traefik` and every :810N URL starts working immediately. Step 2 (later): I open PRs against staging-infra + previewctl to bake the missing pieces (tls.yml committed, certs/ documented as required-local-setup with a `make certs` target, post-start cert load guaranteed via Traefik restart on cert change, gitignore for previews/ + pr-*.yml, fix the rootless socket path via env var). Your bash sessions stop having local diffs that aren't in main.

options.label: Drop the :810N port URLs entirely, use path-based routes on :443
options.description: Change previewctl to write `Host(...) && PathPrefix('/pr-706/') -> web` routes for every PR (it already does this for the develop preview at pr-0.yml). All previews accessible via `https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net/pr-706/`, riding the Tailscale-managed TLS on :443. No certs/, no tls.yml, no per-PR Traefik entryPoints needed in the repo. Bigger architectural change but eliminates the entire class of bullshit. Dashboard links would change.

options.label: Revert ALL local changes, accept that PR previews stop working
options.description: Discard the core.yml diff, delete tls.yml + certs/, leave previewctl on origin/main. Develop URL still works (Tailscale Serve path). Individual PR ports go back to 404 forever — same state as upstream origin/main. Honest about what the repo actually delivers out of the box.

content.content: Your questions have been answered: "Which direction matches what you actually want?"="Just restart Traefik now, fix repo drift later (Recommended)". You can now continue with these answers in mind.

answers.Which direction matches what you actuall: Just restart Traefik now, fix repo drift later (Recommended)

content.text:
Here's the exact command. The rootless docker daemon runs as `gaia` (uid 1000), so you need to switch to that user.

```bash
sudo -iu gaia bash -lc 'docker restart gaia-staging-traefik-1'
```

That's it. ~2 seconds. After it returns, verify:

```bash
# from your laptop — TLS should now serve a real cert
curl -sI --max-time 5 https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:8102/ | head -1
# expect: HTTP/2 200  (or 307 redirect from Next.js)
```

If 200/redirect: every PR card on `/previews` now works. If still TLS handshake fail, send me the error and I'll keep digging — most likely cause then would be tls.yml syntax rejected silently, which Traefik logs would confirm via `sudo -iu gaia bash -lc 'docker logs --tail 50 gaia-staging-traefik-1'`.

## What I'll do next for the "fix repo drift" half

Two PRs, no changes to your server while I draft them. **Want me to start them now** (in the background while you run the restart), or wait until you confirm `:8102` works?

Just to scope what would be in them:

**PR 1 — `theexperiencecompany/staging-infra`**
- Commit `traefik/dynamic/tls.yml` (the file is generic, no secrets — safe to commit)
- Add `traefik/certs/.gitkeep` + `traefik/certs/.gitignore` (`*.pem` ignored, dir tracked)
- Add `previews/` and `traefik/dynamic/pr-*.yml` to `.gitignore` (they're auto-generated)
- Replace the hard-coded `/var/run/docker.sock` in `compose/core.yml` with `${DOCKER_SOCKET:-/var/run/docker.sock}` so rootless deployments don't need a local diff
- Add `scripts/renew-cert.sh` (the one I drafted) + a `cron.d` snippet that runs every 60 days and `docker restart`s Traefik after
- Update README's "fresh machine setup" with a 3-line cert step

**PR 2 — `theexperiencecompany/gaia-previewctl`**
- Rebase your `feat/previews-table-layout` branch onto `origin/main` (the github-status feature is already merged there — your local edits are dead code)
- Touch a sentinel file (e.g. `traefik/dynamic/.reload`) on every poll cycle so Traefik file-provider watch reliably re-loads even when bind-mount inotify is flaky in rootless docker — this prevents the exact bug you just hit from recurring next time someone redeploys
- Add a post-deploy smoke check: after `docker compose up -d`, curl `https://host:${PR_PORT}/health` and fail loudly. The current code only checks the *container* health, not that Traefik can route to it — that's the gap that let this ship broken

I'll run those in worktrees so they don't touch anything you have open. Say the word.

# Default TLS certificate for all routers declaring `tls: {}`.
#
# Used by the per-PR Traefik routes generated by previewctl (and any other
# router on entryPoints pr8101-pr8120 that asks for TLS).
#
# The cert + key files are not in the repo — they are provisioned per
# environment by `scripts/renew-cert.sh` (see that script's header for the
# setup walkthrough). On Tailscale-fronted nodes, the cert is issued by
# Let's Encrypt via Tailscale and the script fetches it with `tailscale cert`.
tls:
  certificates:
    - certFile: /etc/traefik/certs/cert.pem
      keyFile: /etc/traefik/certs/key.pem
  stores:
    default:
      defaultCertificate:
        certFile: /etc/traefik/certs/cert.pem
        keyFile: /etc/traefik/certs/key.pem

      - --configFile=/etc/traefik/traefik.yml
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:${TRAEFIK_WEB_LOCAL_PORT:-18081}:80"
      - "127.0.0.1:${TRAEFIK_DASHBOARD_PORT:-18080}:8080"
      # Per-PR preview ports — exposed on all interfaces so Tailscale
      # clients can reach https://hostname:810x directly with TLS.
      - "8101-8120:8101-8120"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
      - ../traefik/traefik.yml:/etc/traefik/traefik.yml:ro
      - ../traefik/dynamic:/etc/traefik/dynamic:ro
      - ../traefik/certs:/etc/traefik/certs:ro
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.http.routers.traefik.rule=Host(`${TRAEFIK_DASHBOARD_HOST}`)
      - traefik.http.routers.traefik.entrypoints=web
      - traefik.http.routers.traefik.service=api@internal
      - traefik.http.routers.traefik.middlewares=secure-headers@file
    networks:
      - gaia_core_net

  dozzle:
    image: amir20/dozzle:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      DOZZLE_BASE: /logs
      DOZZLE_NO_ANALYTICS: "true"
      DOZZLE_ENABLE_ACTIONS: "true"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
    networks:

name: gaia-staging

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB}
      TZ: ${TZ}
    command:
      - postgres
      - -c
      - password_encryption=scram-sha-256
    volumes:
      - pg_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:${POSTGRES_LOCAL_PORT:-15432}:5432"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER} -d ${POSTGRES_DB}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - gaia_core_net

  mongo:
    image: mongo:8
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME: ${MONGO_ROOT_USERNAME}
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${MONGO_ROOT_PASSWORD}
      TZ: ${TZ}
    command:
      - mongod
      - --bind_ip_all
    volumes:
      - mongo_data:/data/db
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:${MONGO_LOCAL_PORT:-27018}:27017"
    healthcheck:
      test:
        [
          "CMD-SHELL",
          "mongosh --quiet --username ${MONGO_ROOT_USERNAME} --password ${MONGO_ROOT_PASSWORD} --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'db.adminCommand({ ping: 1 }).ok' | grep 1",
        ]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10
    networks:
      - gaia_core_net

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    # Use an ACL file (persisted in the data volume) as the source of truth for
    # auth, so per-app ACL users created via `ACL SETUSER` survive container
    # restarts. `--requirepass` and `--aclfile` are mutually exclusive at
    # startup, so we seed the default user into users.acl from REDIS_PASSWORD
    # on first boot, then exec redis-server.
    entrypoint:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        if [ ! -s /data/users.acl ]; then
          printf 'user default on >%s ~* &* +@all\n' "$$REDIS_PASSWORD" > /data/users.acl
          chmod 600 /data/users.acl
        fi
        exec redis-server --appendonly yes --aclfile /data/users.acl --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
    environment:
      REDIS_PASSWORD: ${REDIS_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:${REDIS_LOCAL_PORT:-16379}:6379"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "redis-cli -a ${REDIS_PASSWORD} ping | grep PONG"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - gaia_core_net

  rabbitmq:
    image: rabbitmq:4.1-management
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER: ${RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER}
      RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS: ${RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS}
      TZ: ${TZ}
    volumes:
      - rabbitmq_data:/var/lib/rabbitmq
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:${RABBITMQ_LOCAL_PORT:-15673}:5672"
      - "127.0.0.1:${RABBITMQ_MGMT_LOCAL_PORT:-15674}:15672"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "rabbitmq-diagnostics check_running"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10
    networks:
      - gaia_core_net

  chromadb:
    image: chromadb/chroma:1.5.1
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      IS_PERSISTENT: "TRUE"
      PERSIST_DIRECTORY: /chroma/chroma
    volumes:
      - chroma_data:/chroma/chroma
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:${CHROMADB_LOCAL_PORT:-18081}:8000"
    healthcheck:
      test:
        [
          "CMD-SHELL",
          "bash -c 'cat < /dev/null > /dev/tcp/localhost/8000'",
        ]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10
    networks:
      - gaia_core_net

  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.1
    restart: unless-stopped
    command:
      - --configFile=/etc/traefik/traefik.yml
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:${TRAEFIK_WEB_LOCAL_PORT:-18081}:80"
      - "127.0.0.1:${TRAEFIK_DASHBOARD_PORT:-18080}:8080"
      # Per-PR preview ports — exposed on all interfaces so Tailscale
      # clients can reach https://hostname:810x directly with TLS.
      - "8101-8120:8101-8120"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
      - ../traefik/traefik.yml:/etc/traefik/traefik.yml:ro
      - ../traefik/dynamic:/etc/traefik/dynamic:ro
      - ../traefik/certs:/etc/traefik/certs:ro
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.http.routers.traefik.rule=Host(`${TRAEFIK_DASHBOARD_HOST}`)
      - traefik.http.routers.traefik.entrypoints=web
      - traefik.http.routers.traefik.service=api@internal
      - traefik.http.routers.traefik.middlewares=secure-headers@file
    networks:
      - gaia_core_net

  dozzle:
    image: amir20/dozzle:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      DOZZLE_BASE: /logs
      DOZZLE_NO_ANALYTICS: "true"
      DOZZLE_ENABLE_ACTIONS: "true"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
    networks:
      - gaia_core_net

  previews:
    image: nginx:alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ../previews:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro
    networks:
      - gaia_core_net

  cloudflared:
    image: cloudflare/cloudflared:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    profiles:
      - public
    command: tunnel --config /etc/cloudflared/config.yml run
    depends_on:
      - traefik
    volumes:
      - ${CLOUDFLARED_CONFIG_FILE:-../cloudflared/config.yml}:/etc/cloudflared/config.yml:ro
      - ${CLOUDFLARED_CREDENTIALS_FILE:-../cloudflared/credentials.json}:/etc/cloudflared/credentials.json:ro
    networks:
      - gaia_core_net

volumes:
  pg_data:
  mongo_data:
  redis_data:
  rabbitmq_data:
  chroma_data:

networks:
  gaia_core_net:
    name: gaia_core_net

content.description: Open PR against theexperiencecompany/staging-infra: (a) commit traefik/dynamic/tls.yml (no secrets), (b) add traefik/certs/.gitignore so dir is tracked but pem files aren't, (c) gitignore previews/ and traefik/dynamic/pr-*.yml (previewctl-generated), (d) replace hardcoded /var/run/docker.sock with ${DOCKER_SOCKET:-/var/run/docker.sock} in compose/core.yml, (e) add scripts/renew-cert.sh + systemd timer drop-in that runs every 60 days and restarts Traefik after. Update README's setup section. Use isolated worktree.

content.subject: PR2 previewctl: post-deploy smoke check + Traefik reload trigger
content.description: Open PR against theexperiencecompany/gaia-previewctl: (a) after `docker compose up -d` in docker_ops, curl https://${PUBLIC_ORIGIN}/health with retries; fail loudly if Traefik can't route; (b) touch a sentinel file traefik/dynamic/.reload after every poll cycle so rootless bind-mount inotify gaps don't strand new routes. Use isolated worktree off origin/main.

content.text: Now the cert renewal script.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Fetch a TLS cert for this node and write it where Traefik reads from.
#
# Today only Tailscale-fronted nodes are supported: `tailscale cert` produces
# a Let's Encrypt-signed cert valid for ~90 days. Cert/key files are written
# to traefik/certs/ (Traefik bind-mounts that as :ro at /etc/traefik/certs).
#
# Usage:
#   sudo bash scripts/renew-cert.sh                 # cert hostname auto-detected
#   sudo PUBLIC_HOST=host.ts.net bash …             # explicit hostname override
#
# Optional env:
#   PUBLIC_HOST    — override the cert hostname. Defaults to this node's Tailscale DNSName.
#   CERT_DIR       — output directory (default: <staging-infra>/traefik/certs)
#   TRAEFIK_NAME   — container name to restart after writing certs
#                    (default: gaia-staging-traefik-1; pass empty to skip).
#
# Schedule via systemd: see systemd/cert-renew.{service,timer} for a 60-day
# timer drop-in that runs this script and restarts Traefik automatically.

set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
CERT_DIR="${CERT_DIR:-${ROOT_DIR}/traefik/certs}"
TRAEFIK_NAME="${TRAEFIK_NAME-gaia-staging-traefik-1}"

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "Must be run as root (sudo) — tailscale cert needs to talk to tailscaled." >&2
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v tailscale >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "tailscale CLI not found on PATH." >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [[ -z "${PUBLIC_HOST:-}" ]]; then
  PUBLIC_HOST="$(tailscale status --json \
    | sed -n 's/.*"DNSName"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)\.".*/\1/p' \
    | head -n1)"
  if [[ -z "${PUBLIC_HOST}" ]]; then
    echo "Could not determine this node's Tailscale DNSName." >&2
    echo "Pass PUBLIC_HOST=<host>.ts.net explicitly." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
fi

mkdir -p "${CERT_DIR}"
TMP_CRT="$(mktemp "${CERT_DIR}/.cert.pem.XXXXXX")"
TMP_KEY="$(mktemp "${CERT_DIR}/.key.pem.XXXXXX")"
trap 'rm -f "${TMP_CRT}" "${TMP_KEY}"' EXIT

echo "[renew-cert] Requesting cert for ${PUBLIC_HOST}..."
tailscale cert --cert-file "${TMP_CRT}" --key-file "${TMP_KEY}" "${PUBLIC_HOST}"

# Atomic swap so Traefik never sees a half-written cert.
mv -f "${TMP_CRT}" "${CERT_DIR}/cert.pem"
mv -f "${TMP_KEY}" "${CERT_DIR}/key.pem"
chmod 0644 "${CERT_DIR}/cert.pem"
chmod 0640 "${CERT_DIR}/key.pem"
trap - EXIT

echo "[renew-cert] Wrote ${CERT_DIR}/{cert,key}.pem"

# Traefik's file provider has `watch: true`, but on rootless docker with
# bind-mounted dynamic configs, inotify on new/replaced files can be missed.
# A container restart is fast (~2s) and guarantees the new cert is loaded.
if [[ -n "${TRAEFIK_NAME}" ]] && command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${TRAEFIK_NAME}$"; then
    echo "[renew-cert] Restarting ${TRAEFIK_NAME}..."
    docker restart "${TRAEFIK_NAME}" >/dev/null
  fi
fi

echo "[renew-cert] Done."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Fetch a TLS cert for this node and write it where Traefik reads from.
#
# Today only Tailscale-fronted nodes are supported: `tailscale cert` produces
# a Let's Encrypt-signed cert valid for ~90 days. Cert/key files are written
# to traefik/certs/ (Traefik bind-mounts that as :ro at /etc/traefik/certs).
#
# Usage:
#   sudo bash scripts/renew-cert.sh                 # cert hostname auto-detected
#   sudo PUBLIC_HOST=host.ts.net bash …             # explicit hostname override
#
# Optional env:
#   PUBLIC_HOST    — override the cert hostname. Defaults to this node's Tailscale DNSName.
#   CERT_DIR       — output directory (default: <staging-infra>/traefik/certs)
#   TRAEFIK_NAME   — container name to restart after writing certs
#                    (default: gaia-staging-traefik-1; pass empty to skip).
#
# Schedule via systemd: see systemd/cert-renew.{service,timer} for a 60-day
# timer drop-in that runs this script and restarts Traefik automatically.

set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
CERT_DIR="${CERT_DIR:-${ROOT_DIR}/traefik/certs}"
TRAEFIK_NAME="${TRAEFIK_NAME-gaia-staging-traefik-1}"

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then

fi

if ! command -v tailscale >/dev/null 2>&1; then

fi

    | sed -n 's/.*"DNSName"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)\.".*/\1/p' \
    | head -n1)"

    echo "Could not determine this node's Tailscale DNSName." >&2
    echo "Pass PUBLIC_HOST=<host>.ts.net explicitly." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
fi

mkdir -p "${CERT_DIR}"
TMP_CRT="$(mktemp "${CERT_DIR}/.cert.pem.XXXXXX")"
TMP_KEY="$(mktemp "${CERT_DIR}/.key.pem.XXXXXX")"
trap 'rm -f "${TMP_CRT}" "${TMP_KEY}"' EXIT

echo "[renew-cert] Requesting cert for ${PUBLIC_HOST}..."
tailscale cert --cert-file "${TMP_CRT}" --key-file "${TMP_KEY}" "${PUBLIC_HOST}"

# Atomic swap so Traefik never sees a half-written cert.
mv -f "${TMP_CRT}" "${CERT_DIR}/cert.pem"
mv -f "${TMP_KEY}" "${CERT_DIR}/key.pem"
chmod 0644 "${CERT_DIR}/cert.pem"
chmod 0640 "${CERT_DIR}/key.pem"

# Traefik's file provider has `watch: true`, but on rootless docker with

# A container restart is fast (~2s) and guarantees the new cert is loaded.
if [[ -n "${TRAEFIK_NAME}" ]] && command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then

    echo "[renew-cert] Restarting ${TRAEFIK_NAME}..."
    docker restart "${TRAEFIK_NAME}" >/dev/null
  fi
fi

echo "[renew-cert] Done."

[Unit]
Description=Renew Tailscale TLS cert every 60 days (Let's Encrypt valid for 90)

[Timer]
# Run 5 min after boot to catch nodes that were offline at the original schedule,
# then every 60 days. RandomizedDelaySec avoids hammering tailscaled across a
# fleet that all booted at once.
OnBootSec=5min
OnUnitActiveSec=60d
RandomizedDelaySec=30min
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target

# staging-infra

Shared staging infrastructure for Gaia services.

This repository owns the common platform layer:
- Traefik ingress (host-based routing)
- Cloudflare Tunnel exposure (no inbound router ports)
- Shared PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, RabbitMQ, and ChromaDB
- Backup and restore scripts
- Self-hosted GitHub Actions deploy workflow

## 1) Prerequisites on server

- Docker Engine + Compose plugin installed and running
- GitHub Actions self-hosted runner configured on this machine with label `staging`
- Cloudflare Tunnel credentials file available at `cloudflared/credentials.json`

## 2) Bootstrap

```bash
./scripts/bootstrap.sh
```

Edit `env/core.env` and `cloudflared/config.yml` with real values.

## 3) Deploy core stack

```bash
./scripts/preflight.sh
./scripts/deploy.sh
```

Note: this deploys internal core services. To include Cloudflare Tunnel exposure, run:

```bash
ENABLE_PUBLIC=1 ./scripts/deploy.sh
```

Optional (with observability):

```bash
ENABLE_OBSERVABILITY=1 ./scripts/deploy.sh
```

## 4) Onboard each app (DB + Redis ACL)

```bash
cp env/postgres/_template.env.example env/postgres/<app-name>.env
# Fill values in env/postgres/<app-name>.env
./scripts/provision-app.sh <app-name>
```

`provision-app.sh` creates:
- PostgreSQL role + database for that app
- MongoDB user in shared `GAIA` database (app-scoped user)
- Redis ACL user scoped to app key prefix (includes `INFO` for ARQ startup checks and `KEYS` for cache pattern invalidation)
- RabbitMQ user + vhost permissions for that app
- Chroma collection prefix contract for that app

`onboard-app.sh` remains as a compatibility alias to `provision-app.sh`.

To remove app-scoped resources:

```bash
./scripts/deprovision-app.sh <app-name>
```

## 5) Connect app repositories

Each app repo should join `gaia_core_net` and add Traefik labels.

Example app service snippet:

```yaml
services:
  web:
    image: ghcr.io/your-org/your-app:staging
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - gaia_core_net
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://app1_user:${APP1_DB_PASSWORD}@postgres:5432/app1_staging
      MONGO_DB: mongodb://app1_user:${APP1_MONGO_PASSWORD}@mongo:27017/app1_staging?authSource=app1_staging
      REDIS_URL: redis://app1_redis_user:${APP1_REDIS_PASSWORD}@redis:6379/0
      RABBITMQ_URL: amqp://app1_rabbit_user:${APP1_RABBIT_PASSWORD}@rabbitmq:5672/app1
      CHROMADB_HOST: chromadb
      CHROMADB_PORT: 8000
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.docker.network=gaia_core_net
      - traefik.http.routers.app1.rule=Host(`app1-staging.example.com`)
      - traefik.http.routers.app1.entrypoints=web
      - traefik.http.routers.app1.middlewares=secure-headers@file
      - traefik.http.services.app1.loadbalancer.server.port=3000

networks:
  gaia_core_net:
    external: true
```

## 6) Deploy model

- `staging-infra` deploys shared core via this repo/workflow
- each app repo deploys its own containers to the same host/network
- cloudflared exposes hostnames to internet through Cloudflare Access policies

## 6.1) GitHub Actions secrets for e2e deploy

Set these repository secrets in `staging-infra`:
- `STAGING_CORE_ENV` -> full multiline content of `env/core.env`
- `CLOUDFLARED_CONFIG_YML` -> full multiline content of `cloudflared/config.yml`
- `CLOUDFLARED_CREDENTIALS_JSON` -> tunnel credentials JSON content

Or set all 3 at once:

```bash
./scripts/set-gh-secrets.sh
```

The workflow writes these files at runtime and runs `./scripts/deploy.sh` on the self-hosted runner.

## 7) Backups

Create local dump:

```bash
./scripts/backup.sh
```

Restore from dump:

```bash
./scripts/restore.sh backups/<timestamp>/postgres.sql
```

If `RESTIC_REPOSITORY` and `RESTIC_PASSWORD` are set in `env/core.env`, `backup.sh` also pushes to restic.

## 8) Preflight checks

```bash
./scripts/preflight.sh
```

Checks Docker daemon access, required files, and compose rendering.

## 9) Status

```bash
./scripts/status.sh
```

## 10) Runner setup

Quick setup:

```bash
./scripts/setup-runner.sh
```

Detailed manual steps: `docs/runner-setup.md`.

input.new_string:
## 3) Deploy core stack

```bash
./scripts/preflight.sh
./scripts/deploy.sh
```

Note: this deploys internal core services. To include Cloudflare Tunnel exposure, run:

```bash
ENABLE_PUBLIC=1 ./scripts/deploy.sh
```

Optional (with observability):

```bash
ENABLE_OBSERVABILITY=1 ./scripts/deploy.sh
```

### Rootless Docker

If the host runs rootless Docker, set `DOCKER_SOCKET` in `env/core.env` to the
user-namespaced socket path before deploy:

```
DOCKER_SOCKET=/run/user/1000/docker.sock
```

The default is `/var/run/docker.sock` (rootful).

### TLS cert for per-PR preview ports

Per-PR previews are served at `https://<host>:810N` via Traefik entrypoints
`pr8101`–`pr8120`. The routers declare `tls: {}` and pick up a default cert
from `traefik/dynamic/tls.yml`. Provision the actual cert + key with:

```bash
sudo bash scripts/renew-cert.sh         # cert hostname auto-detected from tailscaled
```

On Tailscale-fronted nodes this fetches a Let's Encrypt cert via
`tailscale cert` and restarts Traefik. Re-run every ~60 days, or install the
provided systemd timer for unattended renewal:

```bash
sudo install -m 644 systemd/cert-renew.service /etc/systemd/system/
sudo install -m 644 systemd/cert-renew.timer   /etc/systemd/system/
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable --now cert-renew.timer
```

If your clone lives somewhere other than `/home/gaia/gaia-staging/staging-infra`,
override the path with `sudo systemctl edit cert-renew.service` and set
`STAGING_INFRA_DIR=` to your actual path.

```yaml
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.docker.network=gaia_core_net
      - traefik.http.routers.app1.rule=Host(`app1-staging.example.com`)
      - traefik.http.routers.app1.entrypoints=web
      - traefik.http.routers.app1.middlewares=secure-headers@file
      - traefik.http.services.app1.loadbalancer.server.port=3000

networks:
  gaia_core_net:
    external: true
```

## 6) Deploy model

- `staging-infra` deploys shared core via this repo/workflow
- each app repo deploys its own containers to the same host/network
- cloudflared exposes hostnames to internet through Cloudflare Access policies

## 6.1) GitHub Actions secrets for e2e deploy

Set these repository secrets in `staging-infra`:
- `STAGING_CORE_ENV` -> full multiline content of `env/core.env`
- `CLOUDFLARED_CONFIG_YML` -> full multiline content of `cloudflared/config.yml`
- `CLOUDFLARED_CREDENTIALS_JSON` -> tunnel credentials JSON content

Or set all 3 at once:

```bash
./scripts/set-gh-secrets.sh
```

The workflow writes these files at runtime and runs `./scripts/deploy.sh` on the self-hosted runner.

## 7) Backups

Create local dump:

```bash
./scripts/backup.sh
```

Restore from dump:

```bash
./scripts/restore.sh backups/<timestamp>/postgres.sql
```

If `RESTIC_REPOSITORY` and `RESTIC_PASSWORD` are set in `env/core.env`, `backup.sh` also pushes to restic.

## 8) Preflight checks

```bash
./scripts/preflight.sh
```

Checks Docker daemon access, required files, and compose rendering.

## 9) Status

```bash
./scripts/status.sh
```

## 10) Runner setup

Quick setup:

```bash
./scripts/setup-runner.sh
```

Detailed manual steps: `docs/runner-setup.md`.

input.command:
cd /tmp/work/staging-infra && git -c user.email=joint.access01@gmail.com -c user.name=Aryan commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(traefik): commit TLS dynamic config + ship rootless-friendly defaults

The per-PR preview ports (8101-8120) are designed to terminate TLS at
Traefik via the routers previewctl generates (each declaring `tls: {}`),
but the supporting bits were never in the repo:

- the TLS store + default certificate config (`traefik/dynamic/tls.yml`)
- a cert-provisioning script + systemd timer for unattended renewal
- a way to swap the Docker socket path for rootless installs

Without them, every clone has to grow the same local hacks to make HTTPS
work on those ports — and any single Traefik restart on a node where
those hacks were applied but never committed silently breaks the port
URLs again (this is exactly the failure mode we just hit).

Changes:

- `traefik/dynamic/tls.yml`: declare default cert at /etc/traefik/certs/
  for any router asking for `tls: {}`. No secrets in the file itself.
- `traefik/certs/.gitignore`: track the directory, ignore `*.pem`.
- `previews/.gitignore`: track the nginx mount target dir, ignore the
  index.html that previewctl rewrites every poll cycle.
- `compose/core.yml`: parameterize the docker socket path via
  `${DOCKER_SOCKET:-/var/run/docker.sock}`. Rootless hosts now just set
  `DOCKER_SOCKET=/run/user/<uid>/docker.sock` in env/core.env instead of
  editing the compose file.
- `scripts/renew-cert.sh`: fetch a Let's Encrypt cert via `tailscale
  cert`, atomic-swap the .pem files, restart Traefik so the new cert is
  picked up immediately (Traefik file-provider watch is unreliable on
  bind-mounted rootless docker volumes — explicit restart is cheap and
  deterministic).
- `systemd/cert-renew.{service,timer}`: 60-day timer drop-in so renewal
  happens without operator involvement (Let's Encrypt certs are valid 90
  days; 60d cadence gives a 30d safety margin).
- `README.md`: document the rootless override and the cert setup as
  one-time install steps.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

input.command:
cd /tmp/work/staging-infra && gh pr create --base main --title "fix(traefik): commit TLS dynamic config + ship rootless-friendly defaults" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

The per-PR preview ports (`8101-8120`) are designed to terminate TLS at Traefik via the routers `previewctl` generates (each declaring `tls: {}`), but the supporting bits were never in the repo:

- the TLS store + default certificate config (`traefik/dynamic/tls.yml`)
- a cert-provisioning script + systemd timer for unattended renewal
- a way to swap the Docker socket path for rootless installs

Without them, every clone grows the same local hacks to make HTTPS work on those ports. And any single Traefik restart on a node where those hacks were applied but never committed silently breaks the port URLs again — **this is exactly the failure mode we just hit on `gaia-home-server`**: Traefik was started before a locally-written `tls.yml` was added, file-provider watch missed the new file under a rootless bind mount, and the per-PR URLs began handshake-failing with \`TLS alert: unrecognized name\` until someone manually \`docker restart gaia-staging-traefik-1\`'d.

## Changes

- **\`traefik/dynamic/tls.yml\`**: declare default cert at \`/etc/traefik/certs/\` for any router asking for \`tls: {}\`. Contains no secrets.
- **\`traefik/certs/.gitignore\`**: track the directory (Traefik bind-mounts it), ignore \`*.pem\`.
- **\`previews/.gitignore\`**: track the nginx mount target dir, ignore the \`index.html\` previewctl rewrites every poll cycle.
- **\`compose/core.yml\`**: parameterize the docker socket path via \`\${DOCKER_SOCKET:-/var/run/docker.sock}\`. Rootless hosts set \`DOCKER_SOCKET=/run/user/<uid>/docker.sock\` in \`env/core.env\` instead of patching the compose file.
- **\`scripts/renew-cert.sh\`**: fetch a Let's Encrypt cert via \`tailscale cert\`, atomic-swap the .pem files, restart Traefik so the cert is picked up immediately (the file-provider watch is unreliable on bind-mounted rootless docker volumes — explicit restart is cheap and deterministic).
- **\`systemd/cert-renew.{service,timer}\`**: 60-day timer drop-in so renewal is unattended (LE certs are valid 90 days; 60d gives a 30d safety margin).
- **\`README.md\`**: document the rootless override and cert setup as one-time install steps.

## Test plan

- [ ] On \`gaia-home-server\`: \`git pull\`, set \`DOCKER_SOCKET\` in \`env/core.env\`, drop the local \`compose/core.yml\` and \`traefik/dynamic/tls.yml\` diffs (now in main), confirm \`docker compose up -d traefik dozzle\` brings them up clean.
- [ ] \`sudo bash scripts/renew-cert.sh\` produces \`traefik/certs/cert.pem\` + \`key.pem\`, restarts Traefik, and \`curl -sI https://gaia-home-server.taila76294.ts.net:8102/\` returns 200.
- [ ] Install the systemd timer, verify \`systemctl status cert-renew.timer\` shows next-fire time ~60 days out.
- [ ] On a fresh clone elsewhere, follow the README steps end-to-end without referring to chat history or local hacks.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

def docker_up_preview(
    spec: DockerPreviewSpec,
    manifest: dict[str, str],
) -> str:
    """Provision resources, render compose, start the per-PR stack. Returns the preview URL."""
    preview_url = _public_origin(spec)

    resource_env = render_resource_env(spec.runtime_dir, manifest, preview_url)
    _copy_resource_env_to_staging(resource_env, spec.staging_infra_dir, spec.pr_number)

    script_env = _build_script_env(spec)
    _run(
        [
            "bash",
            "scripts/docker-up.sh",
            str(spec.pr_number),
            spec.api_image,
            spec.web_image,
        ],
        workdir=spec.staging_infra_dir,
        env=script_env,
    )
    return preview_url


def docker_down_preview(
    spec: DockerPreviewSpec,
    drop_data: bool = False,
) -> None:
    """Tear down the per-PR stack and deprovision shared resources."""
    args = ["bash", "scripts/docker-down.sh", str(spec.pr_number)]
    if drop_data:
        args.append("--drop-data")
    _run(args, workdir=spec.staging_infra_dir, env=_build_script_env(spec))

    staging_app_env = (
        spec.staging_infra_dir / "env" / "postgres" / f"pr-{spec.pr_number}.env"
    )
    if staging_app_env.exists():
        staging_app_env.unlink()


def docker_stack_status(spec: DockerPreviewSpec) -> dict[str, str]:
    """Return container name → health/state map for the PR stack."""
    project = f"gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}"
    result = subprocess.run(
        [
            "docker",
            "ps",
            "--filter",
            f"label=com.docker.compose.project={project}",
            "--format",
            "{{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}",
        ],
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
        check=False,
    )
    out: dict[str, str] = {}
    for line in result.stdout.splitlines():
        if "\t" not in line:
            continue
        name, status = line.split("\t", 1)
        out[name.strip()] = status.strip()
    return out


def write_traefik_route(spec: DockerPreviewSpec) -> Path:
    """Write a Traefik file-provider route for this PR. Idempotent."""
    traefik_dir = spec.staging_infra_dir / "traefik" / "dynamic"
    traefik_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    target = traefik_dir / f"pr-{spec.pr_number}.yml"

    if spec.pr_port > 0:
        # Port-based routing: dedicated entrypoint per PR, path-prefix routing.
        # TLS is terminated by Traefik using the cert in the default TLS store.
        entrypoint = f"pr{spec.pr_port}"
        # Optional extra routers on the plain 'web' entrypoint so the bare
        # domain (Tailscale Serve → :80) also reaches this preview.
        web_routers = ""
        if spec.add_web_entrypoint_route and spec.public_host_override:
            h = spec.public_host_override
            web_routers = f"""    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api-web:
      rule: "Host(`{h}`) && PathPrefix(`/api`)"
      entryPoints: [web]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-api
      priority: 10
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web-web:
      rule: "Host(`{h}`)"
      entryPoints: [web]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-web
      priority: 1
"""
        content = f"""# auto-generated by previewctl for PR {spec.pr_number} on port {spec.pr_port}
http:
  routers:
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:
      rule: "PathPrefix(`/api`)"
      entryPoints: [{entrypoint}]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-api
      tls: {{}}
      priority: 10
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:
      rule: "PathPrefix(`/`)"
      entryPoints: [{entrypoint}]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-web
      tls: {{}}
      priority: 1
{web_routers}  services:
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:3000"
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:8000"
"""
    else:
        # Legacy host-based routing on the shared :80 entrypoint.
        host = _public_host(spec)
        content = f"""# auto-generated by previewctl for PR {spec.pr_number}
http:
  routers:
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:
      rule: "Host(`{host}`)"
      entryPoints: [web]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-web
      priority: 1
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:
      rule: "Host(`{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/api`)"
      entryPoints: [web]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-api
      priority: 10
  services:
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:3000"
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:8000"
"""
    target.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
    return target


def remove_traefik_route(spec: DockerPreviewSpec) -> None:
    target = (
        spec.staging_infra_dir / "traefik" / "dynamic" / f"pr-{spec.pr_number}.yml"
    )
    if target.exists():
        target.unlink()


__all__ = [
    "DockerPreviewSpec",
    "docker_down_preview",
    "docker_stack_status",
    "docker_up_preview",
    "remove_traefik_route",
    "write_env_file",
    "write_traefik_route",
]

"""
Docker-based preview lifecycle.

Parallel to the PM2-based flow in ops.py, this module brings up a per-PR
stack using pre-built container images. It reuses the existing resource
manifest (resource.py) and staging-infra provisioning scripts so DB/Mongo/
Redis/RabbitMQ user isolation is identical to the host-based flow.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import shutil
import subprocess
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path

from previewctl.ops import render_resource_env, write_env_file


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DockerPreviewSpec:
    pr_number: int
    api_image: str
    web_image: str

help="Path to staging-infra checkout (default: from config).",
    ),
) -> None:
    """Bring up a Docker-based preview stack for a PR."""
    from previewctl.docker_ops import (
        DockerPreviewSpec,
        docker_up_preview,
        write_traefik_route,
    )
    from previewctl.port_pool import PORT_RANGE_START, PortPool

    cfg = load_config()
    host_override = public_host.strip()
    domain = public_base_domain.strip() or cfg.public_base_domain
    if not domain and not host_override:
        raise typer.BadParameter(
            "public_base_domain or public_host is required "
            "(via --public-base-domain, --public-host, or PREVIEWCTL_PUBLIC_BASE_DOMAIN)"
        )

    pool = PortPool(cfg.runtime_dir / "docker-port-pool.json")
    assigned_port = port if port > 0 else pool.allocate(pr)
    typer.echo(f"[pr-{pr}] assigned port {assigned_port}")

    spec = DockerPreviewSpec(
        pr_number=pr,
        api_image=api_image,
        web_image=web_image,
        staging_infra_dir=(staging_infra_dir or cfg.staging_infra_dir).resolve(),
        runtime_dir=cfg.runtime_dir,
        public_base_domain=domain,
        public_scheme=public_scheme or cfg.public_scheme,
        core_network=cfg.compose_network,
        public_host_override=host_override,
        pr_port=assigned_port,
    )

    slot = assigned_port - PORT_RANGE_START
    manifest = build_resource_manifest(pr, slot)
    write_traefik_route(spec)
    preview_url = docker_up_preview(spec, manifest)
    typer.echo(f"Preview ready: {preview_url}")


@app.command("docker-down")
def cli_docker_down(
    pr: int = typer.Option(..., "--pr", help="PR number"),
    drop_data: bool = typer.Option(
        False,
        "--drop-data",
        help="Also drop Postgres DB, Mongo DB, and RabbitMQ vhost (default: keep data).",
    ),
    staging_infra_dir: Path = typer.Option(
        None,
        "--staging-infra-dir",
        help="Path to staging-infra checkout (default: from config).",
    ),
) -> None:
    """Tear down a Docker-based preview stack and deprovision its resources."""
    from previewctl.docker_ops import (
        DockerPreviewSpec,
        docker_down_preview,
        remove_traefik_route,
    )
    from previewctl.port_pool import PortPool

    cfg = load_config()
    pool = PortPool(cfg.runtime_dir / "docker-port-pool.json")

    spec = DockerPreviewSpec(
        pr_number=pr,
        api_image="",
        web_image="",
        staging_infra_dir=(staging_infra_dir or cfg.staging_infra_dir).resolve(),
        runtime_dir=cfg.runtime_dir,

def _docker_deploy_pr(
    cfg: object,
    pr_number: int,
    sha: str,
    api_image: str,
    web_image: str,
    public_host: str,
) -> str:
    """Deploy or redeploy a Docker PR preview. Returns the preview URL."""
    from previewctl.docker_ops import (
        DockerPreviewSpec,
        docker_down_preview,
        docker_stack_status,
        docker_up_preview,
        remove_traefik_route,
        write_traefik_route,
    )
    from previewctl.port_pool import PORT_RANGE_START, PortPool

    pool = PortPool(cfg.runtime_dir / "docker-port-pool.json")
    assigned_port = pool.allocate(pr_number)

    # Tear down existing stack for this PR before redeploying
    status = docker_stack_status(
        DockerPreviewSpec(
            pr_number=pr_number,
            api_image="",
            web_image="",
            staging_infra_dir=cfg.staging_infra_dir,
            runtime_dir=cfg.runtime_dir,
            public_base_domain=cfg.public_base_domain or "",
            public_scheme=cfg.public_scheme,
            core_network=cfg.compose_network,
            public_host_override=public_host,
            pr_port=assigned_port,
        )
    )
    if status:
        typer.echo(f"[pr-{pr_number}] Tearing down existing stack before redeploy")
        old_spec = DockerPreviewSpec(
            pr_number=pr_number,
            api_image="",
            web_image="",
            staging_infra_dir=cfg.staging_infra_dir,
            runtime_dir=cfg.runtime_dir,
            public_base_domain=cfg.public_base_domain or "",
            public_scheme=cfg.public_scheme,
            core_network=cfg.compose_network,
            public_host_override=public_host,
            pr_port=assigned_port,
        )
        remove_traefik_route(old_spec)
        docker_down_preview(old_spec)

    spec = DockerPreviewSpec(
        pr_number=pr_number,
        api_image=api_image,
        web_image=web_image,
        staging_infra_dir=cfg.staging_infra_dir,
        runtime_dir=cfg.runtime_dir,
        public_base_domain=cfg.public_base_domain or "",
        public_scheme=cfg.public_scheme,
        core_network=cfg.compose_network,
        public_host_override=public_host,
        pr_port=assigned_port,
        add_web_entrypoint_route=(pr_number == getattr(cfg, "develop_pr_number", 0)),
    )
    slot = assigned_port - PORT_RANGE_START
    manifest = build_resource_manifest(pr_number, slot)
    write_traefik_route(spec)
    return docker_up_preview(spec, manifest)


def _docker_teardown_pr(cfg: object, pr_number: int, public_host: str) -> None:

        DockerPreviewSpec,
        docker_down_preview,
        remove_traefik_route,
    )

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""
Docker-based preview lifecycle.

Parallel to the PM2-based flow in ops.py, this module brings up a per-PR
stack using pre-built container images. It reuses the existing resource
manifest (resource.py) and staging-infra provisioning scripts so DB/Mongo/
Redis/RabbitMQ user isolation is identical to the host-based flow.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import shutil
import subprocess
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path

from previewctl.ops import render_resource_env, write_env_file


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DockerPreviewSpec:
    pr_number: int
    api_image: str
    web_image: str
    staging_infra_dir: Path
    runtime_dir: Path
    public_base_domain: str
    public_scheme: str = "https"
    core_network: str = "gaia_core_net"
    # When set, preview uses this as its full public host instead of
    # pr-{N}.{public_base_domain}. Use for single-preview-at-a-time setups
    # where the preview lives at the root domain (e.g. Tailscale).
    public_host_override: str = ""
    # When > 0, preview is served on this dedicated port via a Traefik
    # entrypoint named pr{port}. PUBLIC_ORIGIN becomes scheme://host:port.
    pr_port: int = 0
    # When True and public_host_override is set, also write a host-based route
    # on the 'web' entrypoint so the plain domain (via Tailscale Serve → :80)
    # reaches this preview in addition to the port-based TLS route.
    add_web_entrypoint_route: bool = False


def _run(cmd: list[str], workdir: Path, env: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> None:
    merged = os.environ.copy()
    if env:
        merged.update(env)
    subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=str(workdir), env=merged, check=True)


def _copy_resource_env_to_staging(
    resource_env: Path,
    staging_infra_dir: Path,
    pr_number: int,
) -> Path:
    target = staging_infra_dir / "env" / "postgres" / f"pr-{pr_number}.env"
    target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    shutil.copyfile(resource_env, target)
    target.chmod(0o600)
    return target


def _public_host(spec: DockerPreviewSpec) -> str:
    if spec.public_host_override:
        return spec.public_host_override
    if not spec.public_base_domain:
        raise RuntimeError(
            "public_base_domain or public_host_override is required for docker previews"
        )
    return f"pr-{spec.pr_number}.{spec.public_base_domain}"


def _public_origin(spec: DockerPreviewSpec) -> str:
    host = _public_host(spec)
    if spec.pr_port > 0:
        return f"{spec.public_scheme}://{host}:{spec.pr_port}"
    return f"{spec.public_scheme}://{host}"


def _load_core_env(staging_infra_dir: Path) -> dict[str, str]:
    path = staging_infra_dir / "env" / "core.env"
    out: dict[str, str] = {}
    if not path.exists():
        raise RuntimeError(f"missing core env: {path}")
    for raw in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
        line = raw.strip()
        if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
            continue
        key, value = line.split("=", 1)
        out[key.strip()] = value.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
    return out


def _build_script_env(spec: DockerPreviewSpec) -> dict[str, str]:
    env = {
        "PUBLIC_BASE_DOMAIN": spec.public_base_domain,
        "PUBLIC_SCHEME": spec.public_scheme,
        "CORE_NETWORK": spec.core_network,
        # Pass the fully-computed origin so render-preview.sh never has to
        # recompute it from parts — eliminates any PR_PORT propagation gap.
        "PUBLIC_ORIGIN": _public_origin(spec),
    }
    if spec.public_host_override:
        env["PUBLIC_HOST_OVERRIDE"] = spec.public_host_override
    if spec.pr_port > 0:
        env["PR_PORT"] = str(spec.pr_port)
    for key in (
        "INFISICAL_TOKEN",
        "INFISICAL_PROJECT_ID",
        "INFISICAL_MACHINE_IDENTITY_CLIENT_ID",
        "INFISICAL_MACHINE_IDENTITY_CLIENT_SECRET",
    ):
        value = os.environ.get(key, "").strip()
        if value:
            env[key] = value
    return env


def docker_up_preview(
    spec: DockerPreviewSpec,
    manifest: dict[str, str],
) -> str:
    """Provision resources, render compose, start the per-PR stack. Returns the preview URL."""
    preview_url = _public_origin(spec)

    resource_env = render_resource_env(spec.runtime_dir, manifest, preview_url)
    _copy_resource_env_to_staging(resource_env, spec.staging_infra_dir, spec.pr_number)

    script_env = _build_script_env(spec)
    _run(
        [
            "bash",
            "scripts/docker-up.sh",
            str(spec.pr_number),
            spec.api_image,
            spec.web_image,
        ],
        workdir=spec.staging_infra_dir,
        env=script_env,
    )
    return preview_url


def docker_down_preview(
    spec: DockerPreviewSpec,
    drop_data: bool = False,
) -> None:
    """Tear down the per-PR stack and deprovision shared resources."""
    args = ["bash", "scripts/docker-down.sh", str(spec.pr_number)]
    if drop_data:
        args.append("--drop-data")
    _run(args, workdir=spec.staging_infra_dir, env=_build_script_env(spec))

    staging_app_env = (
        spec.staging_infra_dir / "env" / "postgres" / f"pr-{spec.pr_number}.env"
    )
    if staging_app_env.exists():
        staging_app_env.unlink()


def docker_stack_status(spec: DockerPreviewSpec) -> dict[str, str]:
    """Return container name → health/state map for the PR stack."""
    project = f"gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}"
    result = subprocess.run(
        [
            "docker",
            "ps",
            "--filter",
            f"label=com.docker.compose.project={project}",
            "--format",
            "{{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}",
        ],
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
        check=False,
    )
    out: dict[str, str] = {}
    for line in result.stdout.splitlines():
        if "\t" not in line:
            continue
        name, status = line.split("\t", 1)
        out[name.strip()] = status.strip()
    return out


def write_traefik_route(spec: DockerPreviewSpec) -> Path:
    """Write a Traefik file-provider route for this PR. Idempotent."""
    traefik_dir = spec.staging_infra_dir / "traefik" / "dynamic"
    traefik_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    target = traefik_dir / f"pr-{spec.pr_number}.yml"

    if spec.pr_port > 0:
        # Port-based routing: dedicated entrypoint per PR, path-prefix routing.
        # TLS is terminated by Traefik using the cert in the default TLS store.
        entrypoint = f"pr{spec.pr_port}"
        # Optional extra routers on the plain 'web' entrypoint so the bare
        # domain (Tailscale Serve → :80) also reaches this preview.
        web_routers = ""
        if spec.add_web_entrypoint_route and spec.public_host_override:
            h = spec.public_host_override
            web_routers = f"""    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api-web:
      rule: "Host(`{h}`) && PathPrefix(`/api`)"
      entryPoints: [web]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-api
      priority: 10
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web-web:
      rule: "Host(`{h}`)"
      entryPoints: [web]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-web
      priority: 1
"""
        content = f"""# auto-generated by previewctl for PR {spec.pr_number} on port {spec.pr_port}
http:
  routers:
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:
      rule: "PathPrefix(`/api`)"
      entryPoints: [{entrypoint}]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-api
      tls: {{}}
      priority: 10
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:
      rule: "PathPrefix(`/`)"
      entryPoints: [{entrypoint}]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-web
      tls: {{}}
      priority: 1
{web_routers}  services:
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:3000"
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:8000"
"""
    else:
        # Legacy host-based routing on the shared :80 entrypoint.
        host = _public_host(spec)
        content = f"""# auto-generated by previewctl for PR {spec.pr_number}
http:
  routers:
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:
      rule: "Host(`{host}`)"
      entryPoints: [web]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-web
      priority: 1
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:
      rule: "Host(`{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/api`)"
      entryPoints: [web]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-api
      priority: 10
  services:
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:3000"
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:8000"
"""
    target.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
    return target


def remove_traefik_route(spec: DockerPreviewSpec) -> None:
    target = (
        spec.staging_infra_dir / "traefik" / "dynamic" / f"pr-{spec.pr_number}.yml"
    )
    if target.exists():
        target.unlink()


__all__ = [
    "DockerPreviewSpec",
    "docker_down_preview",
    "docker_stack_status",
    "docker_up_preview",
    "remove_traefik_route",
    "write_env_file",
    "write_traefik_route",
]

            "ps",
            "--filter",
            f"label=com.docker.compose.project={project}",
            "--format",
            "{{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}",
        ],
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
        check=False,
    )
    out: dict[str, str] = {}
    for line in result.stdout.splitlines():
        if "\t" not in line:
            continue
        name, status = line.split("\t", 1)
        out[name.strip()] = status.strip()
    return out


def write_traefik_route(spec: DockerPreviewSpec) -> Path:
    """Write a Traefik file-provider route for this PR. Idempotent."""
    traefik_dir = spec.staging_infra_dir / "traefik" / "dynamic"
    traefik_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    target = traefik_dir / f"pr-{spec.pr_number}.yml"

    if spec.pr_port > 0:
        # Port-based routing: dedicated entrypoint per PR, path-prefix routing.
        # TLS is terminated by Traefik using the cert in the default TLS store.
        entrypoint = f"pr{spec.pr_port}"
        # Optional extra routers on the plain 'web' entrypoint so the bare
        # domain (Tailscale Serve → :80) also reaches this preview.
        web_routers = ""
        if spec.add_web_entrypoint_route and spec.public_host_override:
            h = spec.public_host_override
            web_routers = f"""    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api-web:
      rule: "Host(`{h}`) && PathPrefix(`/api`)"
      entryPoints: [web]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-api
      priority: 10
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web-web:
      rule: "Host(`{h}`)"
      entryPoints: [web]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-web
      priority: 1
"""
        content = f"""# auto-generated by previewctl for PR {spec.pr_number} on port {spec.pr_port}
http:
  routers:
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:
      rule: "PathPrefix(`/api`)"
      entryPoints: [{entrypoint}]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-api
      tls: {{}}
      priority: 10
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:
      rule: "PathPrefix(`/`)"
      entryPoints: [{entrypoint}]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-web
      tls: {{}}
      priority: 1
{web_routers}  services:
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:3000"
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:8000"
"""
    else:
        # Legacy host-based routing on the shared :80 entrypoint.
        host = _public_host(spec)
        content = f"""# auto-generated by previewctl for PR {spec.pr_number}
http:
  routers:
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:
      rule: "Host(`{host}`)"
      entryPoints: [web]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-web
      priority: 1
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:
      rule: "Host(`{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/api`)"
      entryPoints: [web]
      service: pr-{spec.pr_number}-api
      priority: 10
  services:
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}-web:3000"
    pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://gaia-pr-{spec.pr_number}-api:8000"
"""
    target.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
    return target


def remove_traefik_route(spec: DockerPreviewSpec) -> None:
    target = spec.staging_infra_dir / "traefik" / "dynamic" / f"pr-{spec.pr_number}.yml"
    if target.exists():
        target.unlink()


__all__ = [
    "DockerPreviewSpec",
    "docker_down_preview",
    "docker_stack_status",
    "docker_up_preview",
    "remove_traefik_route",
    "write_env_file",
    "write_traefik_route",
]

"""
Docker-based preview lifecycle.

Parallel to the PM2-based flow in ops.py, this module brings up a per-PR
stack using pre-built container images. It reuses the existing resource
manifest (resource.py) and staging-infra provisioning scripts so DB/Mongo/
Redis/RabbitMQ user isolation is identical to the host-based flow.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
import os
from pathlib import Path
import shutil
import subprocess

from previewctl.ops import render_resource_env, write_env_file


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DockerPreviewSpec:
    pr_number: int
    api_image: str
    web_image: str
    staging_infra_dir: Path
    runtime_dir: Path
    public_base_domain: str
    public_scheme: str = "https"
    core_network: str = "gaia_core_net"
    # When set, preview uses this as its full public host instead of
    # pr-{N}.{public_base_domain}. Use for single-preview-at-a-time setups
    # where the preview lives at the root domain (e.g. Tailscale).
    public_host_override: str = ""
    # When > 0, preview is served on this dedicated port via a Traefik
    # entrypoint named pr{port}. PUBLIC_ORIGIN becomes scheme://host:port.
    pr_port: int = 0
    # When True and public_host_override is set, also write a host-based route
    # on the 'web' entrypoint so the plain domain (via Tailscale Serve → :80)
    # reaches this preview in addition to the port-based TLS route.
    add_web_entrypoint_route: bool = False


def _run(cmd: list[str], workdir: Path, env: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> None:
    merged = os.environ.copy()
    if env:
        merged.update(env)
    subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=str(workdir), env=merged, check=True)
